entities.name: autophagy
entities.description: Core cellular degradation pathway; primary dysfunction causes genetic neurodevelopmental and neurodegenerative disorders
entities.evidence: Primary dysfunction of autophagy due to Mendelian defects affecting core components of the autophagy machinery

entities.name: Alzheimer disease
entities.description: Neurodegenerative disorder abbreviated AD in context of autophagy disorders

entities.name: Parkinson disease
entities.description: Neurodegenerative disorder associated with autophagy defects

entities.name: frontotemporal dementia
entities.description: Neurodegenerative disorder associated with autophagy dysfunction

entities.name: Huntington disease
entities.description: Neurodegenerative disorder abbreviated HD

entities.name: beta-propeller protein associated neurodegeneration
entities.description: BPAN, early-onset neurodegenerative disorder due to WDR45 defects

entities.name: BIN1
entities.description: Bridging integrator 1 gene

entities.name: CHMP2B
entities.description: Charged multivesicular body protein 2B gene

entities.description: Ectopic P-granules autophagy protein 5 homolog gene
entities.evidence: EPG5: ectopic P-granules autophagy protein 5 homolog

entities.name: FIG4
entities.description: FIG4 phosphoinositide 5-phosphatase gene

entities.name: GBA
entities.description: Glucocerebrosidase gene

entities.description: Mechanistic target of rapamycin kinase gene
entities.evidence: MTOR: mechanistic target of rapamycin kinase

entities.name: VCP
entities.description: Valosin-containing protein gene

entities.name: WDR45
entities.description: WD repeat domain 45 gene, defects cause BPAN

entities.name: MTOR
entities.description: Mechanistic target of rapamycin kinase protein, regulates autophagy
entities.evidence: MTOR: mechanistic target of rapamycin kinase

entities.name: spasticity
entities.description: Neurological feature of congenital autophagy disorders
entities.evidence: spasticity and peripheral nerve pathology are prominent neurological features

entities.name: peripheral nerve pathology
entities.description: Neurological manifestation in autophagy disorders
entities.evidence: peripheral nerve pathology are prominent neurological features

entities.name: cerebellar involvement
entities.description: CNS structural abnormality in autophagy disorders

entities.name: myopathy
entities.description: Associated neuromuscular manifestation in several autophagy disorders
entities.evidence: an associated myopathy has been characterized in several conditions

entities.name: lysosomal storage disorders
entities.description: Differential diagnosis for autophagy disorders
entities.evidence: mitochondrial, glycogen and lysosomal storage disorders

entities.name: neurodevelopmental disorders
entities.description: Early-onset manifestation of autophagy dysfunction

relations.evidence: AD: Alzheimer disease [listed among autophagy-related neurodegenerative disorders]
PD: Parkinson disease [listed among autophagy-related disorders]
FTD: frontotemporal dementia [listed among autophagy-related disorders]
HD: Huntington disease [listed among autophagy-related disorders]
BPAN: beta-propeller protein associated neurodegeneration; WDR45: WD repeat domain 45
Primary dysfunction of autophagy...important cause of genetic disease...severe early-onset neurodevelopmental...disorders
spasticity...prominent neurological features, indicating a specific vulnerability of certain neuronal populations to autophagic disturbance
an associated myopathy has been characterized in several conditions
MTOR: mechanistic target of rapamycin kinase [key autophagy regulator]
GBA: glucocerebrosidase [known PD risk gene in autophagy context]
CHMP2B: charged multivesicular body protein 2B; FTD: frontotemporal dementia
